facts.claim: Klaipėda is a city in Lithuania on the Baltic Sea coast.

It is the third-largest city in Lithuania, the fifth-largest city in the Baltic states, the capital of Klaipėda County, the only major seaport in the country and the busiest port in the Baltic states.

Lithuania is marked by low hills and the Aukštojas Hill peak at 294 meters, without mountains or fjords. Vilnius, the capital, features a UNESCO-listed Old Town with Gediminas Castle ruins and St. Anne’s Gothic Church near the rivers Neris and Vilnia.
